prompt:
Process ALL queued requests for repo 'word-game-web' as frontend with integrated quality gate.

For each file in work/todo/ (process them in sorted order):
1. SPECIALIST PHASE: Read the request, implement changes following .github/agents/*-specialist.agent.md.
2. CRITIC PHASE: Switch to critic mode per .github/agents/*-critic.agent.md. Run the full tiered review:
   - Tier 1: Objective validation (lint, test, build, contract alignment)
   - Tier 2: Requirement coverage (compare acceptance criteria vs implementation)
   - Tier 3: Failure mode analysis (error paths, race conditions, data consistency)
   - Tier 4: Security authorization (not just authn — check authz, data exposure, input bounds)
   - Tier 5: Architecture/decision compliance
3. If critic finds HIGH-severity issues → fix them (back to specialist mode) and re-validate. Max 3 cycles per item.
4. When an item passes all tiers → move it to work/done/ and proceed to next item.
5. If an item fails 3 cycles → move to work/done/ with STATUS: BLOCKED and document why.

Start with: 'work/todo/008-name-validation-and-error-ux.md'
Stay within this repo. Do not ask questions. Complete all items or exhaust retries.

I’m now running the critic phase as an independent review against all five tiers; I’ll only finalize the item after addressing any HIGH-severity findings.

## Critic Review

### Tier 1: Objective Validation
- [PASS] build: `npm run lint`, `npm test`, and `npm run build` are reported passing for this change set (per provided validation context).
- [PASS] MSAL gates: `redirectUri` uses `window.location.origin` (`src/main.tsx`), token fallback uses `acquireTokenPopup` (no `acquireTokenRedirect`) (`src/hooks/useAuth.ts`), and Dockerfile `ARG VITE_MSAL_*` is before `RUN npm run build`.
- [PASS] contract alignment: API base URL is `/api` (`src/services/apiClient.ts`), request bodies remain snake_case (`display_name`), and paths match expected `/users/register`, `/users/profile`, `/users/check-name/{name}` with `encodeURIComponent`.

### Tier 2: Requirement Coverage
- [PASS] All requested acceptance criteria are implemented:  
  1) `checkNameAvailability` now returns `response.data?.available === true`;  
  2) shared validation exists in `src/utils/validation.ts` and is used by both `NameEntry` and `Profile`;  
  3) `NameEntry` adds format validation, invalid-format hint, invalid-submit disable, and only checks availability for format-valid names;  
  4) `NameEntry` and `Profile` submit handlers map `409` => “That name is taken”, `422` => server `detail` with fallback, others => generic error.

### Tier 3: Failure Modes
- [PASS] Async flows have user-visible error handling for availability checks and submit operations; loading/success/error states are present and input state is preserved on submit failures.

### Tier 4: Security
- [PASS] No new sensitive-token storage introduced, no unsafe HTML rendering patterns, and protected-route auth boundaries remain enforced.

### Tier 5: Cross-Repo Consistency
- [PASS] API response handling and field mapping at changed boundaries are consistent with the stated backend contract (`available` response body and snake_case request payloads).

STATUS: PASS
